claim: Inter should still be treated as the likely winner on 2026-04-17, but the best post-synthesis estimate is a bit below the 81.5% market price because the swarm’s mild bearish view was directionally consistent across all personas and the remaining uncertainty is concentrated in ordinary regulation-time draw risk plus late lineup availability rather than any overlooked bullish Inter catalyst.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: official settlement source not named with full precision, though 90-minute match-result mechanics are clear
independently_verified_points: All five personas converged on Inter as deserved strong favorite but slightly below market
Contract is a 90-minute win market so draw remains the main No path
Fixture/date/opponent alignment appears consistently verified across upstream sources
No persona found strong match-specific evidence justifying a probability above market
Late lineup news, especially around Inter attacking availability, is the main remaining catalyst
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: No strong independent late-team-news or broad bookmaker screen was verified at synthesis stage.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: Inter may simply be strong enough that 81.5% is fair if key attackers are fully available and lineup concern is stale.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: weight placed on draw risk and unresolved lineup availability near kickoff
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes requires Inter to win the scheduled Serie A match in regulation plus stoppage time; a draw is a full No.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: official lineup and late availability news before 2026-04-17 kickoff
decision_blockers: No synthesis-stage confirmation of final Inter lineup or key-attacker availability
No independently verified broad bookmaker screen preserved beyond upstream artifacts
Small remaining settlement-source wording ambiguity despite clear 90-minute mechanics
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Check official lineups and broad odds consensus on match day before kickoff.

sections.Alpha summary: Market implies 81.5%. My post-synthesis estimate is 0.77 to 0.80. That is a small below-market view, so the edge is marginal rather than obviously actionable. The likely source of mispricing, if any, is mild underweighting of regulation-time draw risk and residual lineup uncertainty in a single-match soccer win market.
sections.Input coverage: Coverage is complete: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view were all available and usable. I checked raw findings against sidecars; the sidecars looked broadly faithful, with some compression around source-quality caveats and exact provenance, but no major distortion. Supporting assumption/evidence artifacts were referenced through the raw findings rather than separately reread because the core synthesis disagreements were already legible. Coverage is complete because no persona was missing and no lane looked unusable, though several lanes were intentionally modest rather than deeply sourced.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The synthesis baseline is the 0.815 market-implied probability captured at 2026-04-14T18:15:16Z. The swarm treated that as an information-rich prior and all lanes only shaded modestly below it rather than calling it badly wrong.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: Final synthesized probability range: 0.77 to 0.80. This keeps Inter as a strong favorite, but not enough to endorse the market’s 0.815 with confidence given the contract’s win-only structure and the still-unverified late lineup state.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: The provisional swarm center was 0.78. My final range is essentially aligned with that center and not a material departure. I did not move back toward market because every lane independently landed below market for similar reasons, and I did not find any fresh synthesis-stage evidence strong enough to overturn that consensus. I also did not move further down because the surviving countercase against the bearish view is real: Inter’s team-strength gap may be large enough that the market is simply fair.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: Slight disagreement with market. Inter probably should be favored heavily, but the current price appears a few points rich relative to the best consolidated view. This is not a strong anti-market call; it is a modest claim that market confidence is slightly high for a regulation-win soccer contract.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Verification quality is medium. What was independently checked at synthesis level was mostly cross-lane consistency, sidecar fidelity against raw findings, and whether any persona had produced a materially different source-of-truth or catalyst interpretation. That process confirmed a genuine consensus: all five lanes clustered between 0.76 and 0.80, all identified draw risk as the main losing path, and none surfaced a strong overlooked pro-Inter catalyst beyond possible positive lineup confirmation. What remained unverified is exactly the highest-value external check: fresh match-day team news and a broad independent odds screen. Because the proposed edge versus market is small, medium verification is enough for a mild below-market stance but not for a high-conviction fade.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: No. I did not meaningfully compress toward market because the swarm’s bearish-vs-market gap was already small and consistently supported across personas. If the swarm had implied a much larger edge, the lack of stronger independent verification would have forced more compression. Here, the edge is only a few points and the cross-lane agreement was enough to preserve that mild below-market range.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The key checkpoint is official lineup and availability confirmation before kickoff on 2026-04-17. The most important catalyst is whether Inter’s attack, especially Lautaro-related availability concerns noted upstream, is clean or degraded. The edge is more likely to compress toward market if Inter are clearly full strength, and more likely to widen slightly against Inter if meaningful absences or rotation are confirmed. Waiting for match-day information would likely improve precision.
sections.Decision blockers: Main blockers are the lack of synthesis-stage confirmation of final lineup/availability, absence of a fresh broad bookmaker comparison preserved in the bundle, and minor but nonzero settlement-source wording ambiguity. None of these overturn the base read, but they do cap confidence and make the current edge look modest.
sections.Implication for the question: This synthesis implies Yes remains more likely than No, but not by as much as the market suggests. Inter should win more often than not; the practical question is whether 81.5% overstates how often a clearly superior team converts a single league match into a regulation win.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas agreed Inter are the deserved favorite. All personas landed slightly below market, with estimates from 0.76 to 0.80. All treated regulation-time draw risk as the main reason not to fully endorse 0.815. Multiple lanes also flagged late lineup or availability news as the only realistic near-term catalyst likely to move the fair price materially.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: Disagreement was low-intensity and mostly about weighting, not direction. Base-rate placed the most weight on structural team-strength priors and came in highest at 0.80. Variant-view and risk-manager put more weight on draw/upset variance and lineup uncertainty, landing at 0.76 and 0.77. Catalyst-hunter was more sensitive than others to Lautaro-related availability signals. This is mainly a timing/weighting disagreement rather than a factual or contract dispute.
sections.Best countercase: The strongest countercase, represented most clearly by market-implied and parts of catalyst-hunter, is that the market is broadly efficient here: Inter’s quality edge is large, no strong anti-Inter evidence was found, and if key attackers are available then 81.5% may be fair or even a touch cheap.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Inter are materially stronger than Cagliari, the match is the scheduled Serie A fixture on 2026-04-17, and the market resolves on a regulation-time win. Contested assumptions: how much ordinary draw risk should pull a strong favorite below an 80%+ price; how much latent lineup uncertainty remains unpriced. Fragile assumptions: no major late Inter absences, no heavy rotation from schedule/motivation factors, and no adverse pre-kickoff market drift from information the swarm missed.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: consistent season-strength gap between Inter and Cagliari, home venue for Inter, strong current-season record/form context, and upstream odds context indicating Inter are widely seen as a heavy favorite. Strongest contradictory evidence: this is a win-only soccer market, so draw risk remains material even for a superior side; several lanes lacked official late lineup confirmation; some availability evidence came from secondary sources. Authoritative/source-of-truth evidence: Polymarket-style contract wording reflected in multiple lanes that settlement depends on the 90-minute result. Mixed evidence: Lautaro/attack availability signals, which matter but were not pinned down with final official confirmation.
sections.Evidence weighting: Most weight went to cross-persona convergence, straightforward contract mechanics, and the broad team-strength/home-field case. I downweighted thin or secondary squad-status signals because they were not official lineup confirmations. I also downweighted any impulse to infer a large edge from generic underdog intuition because no lane produced strong match-specific anti-Inter evidence.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence against the synthesized below-market view is simply that no lane found a decisive negative fact about Inter. If the lineup concern is stale or overstated and the squad is near full strength, then the market may already be correctly pricing the same draw-risk considerations the swarm emphasized.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: The synthesis view is that this is a low-ambiguity regulation-time match-winner contract: Yes if Inter win in the first 90 minutes plus stoppage time, No if they draw or lose. The only remaining ambiguity is the precise named operational settlement source if there were data-feed discrepancies, but that looks minor and unlikely to change the substantive forecast.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: Alpha here would come only from the market being a little too willing to translate broad team superiority into an 81.5% regulation-win probability. That can matter in soccer because the draw bucket is structurally large. But alpha could be destroyed if that reasoning is already fully embedded in market pricing or if late lineup news breaks in Inter’s favor and validates the current price.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: This view would move upward if official lineups or trusted pre-match reporting show Inter close to full strength, especially in attack, and a broader bookmaker screen confirms pricing at or above current levels. It would move downward if key Inter absences, heavy rotation, or notable odds drift against Inter appears before kickoff. Any contract clarification changing the settlement away from ordinary 90-minute win logic would also matter, though that currently looks unlikely.
sections.Highest-value next research: Single highest-value next check: official Inter lineup / reliable pre-match team-news confirmation on match day, paired with a quick broad bookmaker consensus screen.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary source class across the swarm was medium-quality event/contract/fixture material plus public club and structured aggregator context. The most important secondary class was standings/form/availability context from sources like Transfermarkt, ESPN, Wikipedia, and club pages. Evidence independence was medium at best: enough for a low-difficulty sports case, but not enough to fully eliminate lineup-risk uncertainty. Source-of-truth ambiguity was low overall, with only minor residual implementation ambiguity. The synthesis is somewhat bottlenecked by thin match-day-specific sourcing rather than by any missing high-level context.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, additional synthesis-stage verification was attempted conceptually through raw-finding review and cross-lane consistency checks, but external web verification was limited and did not produce a fresh independent override. Cross-lane comparison materially reinforced that the below-market stance is real rather than an artifact of one lane. It also exposed a common weakness: several personas relied on broad context more than decisive late team-news verification.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — strongest articulation of why Inter are the deserved favorite while still staying below market because of regulation draw risk. catalyst-hunter — identified late availability, especially Lautaro-related status, as the main remaining repricing catalyst. market-implied — anchored the synthesis against the 0.815 prior and made the best case that the market may already be approximately efficient. risk-manager — best framed why a win-only soccer contract can still be slightly overpriced even when the favorite is clearly superior, and emphasized draw/upset variance. variant-view — preserved the cleanest minority-pressure argument for a slightly larger below-market discount based on single-match win-conversion limits and lineup uncertainty.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Possible durable lesson: for high-priced soccer favorites, the core synthesis job is often not identifying the better team but checking whether draw risk and lineup uncertainty are underweighted in a win-only contract. Possible underbuilt driver: explicit reusable handling for regulation-only draw risk versus team-strength edge conversion. Possible source-quality lesson: match-day lineup verification is the highest-value incremental check when the market already reflects broad public strength signals. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: no; review later for driver candidate: yes; review later for canon or linkage issue: yes; review later for swarm-method issue: no. Reason: this case repeatedly surfaced the need for a reusable sports driver around regulation-time draw-risk compression and still showed weak canonical linkage for the football entities.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Wait for match-day lineup confirmation and a quick bookmaker-consensus check; absent new negative or positive team news, treat the current synthesis as a mild below-market Yes-lean rather than a strong action signal.
